name: Johnny Guarnieri
abstract: John Albert Guarnieri (March 23, 1917 – January 7, 1985) was an American jazz and stride pianist, born in New York City.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Solo Piano I

works.t: Lady, be Good!: Oh, Lady, Be Good!

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: we formed this little trio... that was with Johnny Guarnieri and Billy Taylor and Teddy Walters.

about_quotes.speaker: Slam Stewart
about_quotes.evidence: I had Johnny Guarnieri playing piano for me also in one of my groups.

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Allen
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (Steve Allen) discusses Benny Goodman and Teddy Wilson. Johnny Guarnieri is only mentioned by the Interviewer to correct the name of Benny Goodman's pianist. The Interviewee does not describe any personal interaction, collaboration, or relationship with Guarnieri, only acknowledging
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: All the recordings that I first listened to, from the first one, which was Just You, Just Me, with Lester Young, Johnny Guarnieri, Sid Catlett, and Slam Stewart.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie Williams
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve been overseas another time with Slam Stewart, Johnny Guarnieri, Pat Flowers and we did a tribute to Fats, it was called a “Tribute to Fats Waller.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie Williams interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part I
about_quotes.evidence: I think Johnny Guarnieri was the player, yeah. They had a thing called “Summit Ridge Drive” where he had this long solo.
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part I interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]
